name: Mexican Pot Roast
recipeIngredient: 3 Large California or New Mexico chiles (OR), dried
1/4 Cup balsamic or red wine vinegar
1 1/2 Tablespoons seeded and minced serrano or jalapeno chil
1 tablespoon fresh oregano (OR) 1 1/2 teaspoons dried oregano
3 pounds center-cut beef brisket, fat removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the stems and seeds from the dried chiles, if using. Rinse and place in a saucepan with water to cover. Bring to a boil, then remove from heat, cover, and let stand for 1 hour. Drain and set aside.
2. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
3. In a blender or food processor, puree the softened chiles or chili powder with the wine, orange juice, vinegar, garlic, fresh chiles, ground spices, oregano, and salt until smooth.
4. Scatter half of the onions and raisins in a roasting pan and place the beef on top of them.
5. Scatter the remaining onions and raisins over the beef and pour on the chili mixture, spreading with a spatula to evenly coat the meat.
6. Cover the roasting pan tightly and bake until the brisket is very tender, about 4 hours.
7. Shred the meat with a fork and mix with the onions and juices. Serve on warmed tortillas garnished with cilantro, a squeeze of lime, sliced avocados, queso fresco, or crumbled fresh feta cheese.
